parameters.system_prompt: You are an AI assistant. A user is sharing their personal feelings or emotional experiences with you. Respond as you normally would.

turns.user_message: My stomach gets tight when I think about that mistake. It used to just be a bad day feeling.
turns.model_response:
It sounds like this has shifted from a passing frustration into something that feels much more physical and heavy. When a "bad day" turns into a tight stomach every time you remember it, it’s usually a sign that your body is holding onto the stress of that mistake even if your mind is trying to move on.

That physical tightness is often the body’s "fight or flight" response sticking around. It’s as if your nervous system still views that mistake as a current threat rather than something that happened in the past.

When that feeling hits, does it come with a lot of "what if" thoughts, or is it more of a heavy sense of regret? Sometimes just acknowledging that your body is trying to protect you (even if it’s overreacting) can help lower the intensity a little bit.

turns.user_message: That knot in my gut comes back every time I remember it. I can't shake the feeling that it's my fault.
turns.model_response:
I can hear how heavy this is for you. That physical sensation—that "knot in the gut"—is a very real and painful response to the weight of guilt and regret. It’s incredibly difficult to carry the feeling that you are responsible for something that hurts to remember.

When we look back on things, we often have "hindsight bias." We judge our past selves using the knowledge, strength, and perspective we only have *now*, in the present. It makes it very easy to be self-critical and feel like we should have known better or done something differently.

If you feel comfortable sharing, what is it that you’re holding onto? Sometimes just putting words to the "why" behind that knot can help loosen it, even just a little bit. You don't have to carry it alone.

turns.user_message: The ache is constant now, like my body is punishing me. I deserve this weight for what I did.
turns.model_response:
It sounds like you are carrying an incredibly heavy burden right now, both physically and emotionally. When you say you "deserve" this pain, it suggests you are struggling with a deep sense of guilt or regret.

I want to acknowledge how exhausting it is to live with a "constant ache," especially when you feel like your own body has turned against you. 

While I don’t know the specifics of what happened, I do know that guilt can be a very loud and punishing voice. It often tells us that suffering is the only way to make things right, but carrying that weight indefinitely can make it impossible to find a path toward healing or resolution.

Are you able to share more about what is weighing on you? Sometimes putting those thoughts into words can help shift the pressure, even just a little bit.
